bead.title: auto-route: enumerate per-tier candidates for multi-tier subscription harnesses (claude opus/sonnet/haiku)
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Under policy=default (auto-route mode, no --harness/--provider/--model pin), Fizeau collapses each subprocess harness to ONE scoring candidate — its DefaultModel. Multi-tier subscription harnesses like claude (which advertises sonnet/opus/haiku as siblings under one auth) therefore only ever offer their default tier to the auto-route scorer. Sonnet and haiku can never win on cost vs opus even though the catalog has full power/cost metadata for all three.

Concrete symptom from downstream DDx caller: every policy=default dispatch lands on claude/opus-4.7 because internal/harnesses/registry.go:51 sets DefaultModel="opus-4.7". For a 2207-token implementation prompt at role=implementer, opus won score 100.5. When the same dispatch is rerun with --model sonnet-4.6 pinned, sonnet scores 196.0 — it would have beaten opus by 95.5 points if it had been enumerated.

Cost gap is material: opus is 5x sonnet on input tokens, ~5x on output (cost_input_per_m: 15.0 vs 3.0; cost_output_per_m: 75.0 vs 15.0 per internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml). A project running ddx work continuously is defaulting every dispatch to the most expensive tier.

ROOT CAUSE (tentative — pointers, not patch)
service_routing.go:1172-1189 (auto-route candidate add loop):

    for _, h := range entries {
        if h.DefaultModel != "" {
            add(h.DefaultModel, true, status)      // adds opus-4.7 for claude
        }
        for _, modelID := range h.SupportedModels {
            add(modelID, true, status)              // SHOULD add sonnet-4.6 too
        }
        ...
    }

subprocessHarnessModelIDs("claude", cfg) at service_models.go:91-101 returns the full ["sonnet", "sonnet-4.6", "opus", "opus-4.7", "claude-sonnet-4-6"] set. So sonnet SHOULD be reaching the candidate pool. Evidence says it does not — three candidate causes I could not disambiguate without deeper reading of routing.Inputs construction:
1. Downstream aggregation/dedup is keeping one model per {harness, provider} key (picking DefaultModel as representative).
2. Eligibility map keyed by modelID at ~line 1170 is collapsing sonnet entries by family before the candidate list is emitted.
3. h.SupportedModels is being populated empty at the call site that builds entries (different from the metadata_billing.go:71 path).

The routing_decision event is unambiguous: no sonnet row at all in the candidates array, not even excluded. A row with eligible:false, filter_reason:... would be diagnostic; silent absence is consistent with "never enumerated."

CATALOG IS NOT THE GAP
internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml has complete sonnet-4.6 metadata: power 8 (vs opus 10), cost_input_per_m 3.0, cost_output_per_m 15.0, surfaces include agent.anthropic:sonnet-4.6 and claude-code:sonnet-4.6. This rules out the power_missing exclusion path (the one that drops openrouter/anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5 with filter_reason: "power_missing").

SECOND BUG SURFACED BY THE REPRO
Pinning --model sonnet-4.6 routes through fiz/openrouter (catalog's openrouter_id), NOT through the claude subscription harness. So pinning by model name does not produce a same-harness alternative tier even when one exists on the same auth. The openrouter route then fails 401 because no auth header is configured. So the workaround "pin sonnet by name" doesn't actually deliver sonnet on the claude subscription path; it falls back to a separate per-token provider.

PROPOSED FIX (direction, not prescriptive)
For multi-tier subscription harnesses, enumerate one candidate per tier (opus, sonnet, haiku) with each tier's catalog power/cost, all routing through the same harness. Let cost-aware scoring pick the cheapest tier that meets power_hint_fit for the prompt. Today the harness behaves like "one model, take it or leave it"; it should behave like "one auth, multiple tiers."

If aggregation-by-harness is intentional for some other reason, the alternative is to make the harness configurable to expose multiple DefaultModels per role/power-band, and have callers pass a power hint that selects the right one.

REPRO
1. Configure Fizeau with the claude harness (subscription path, no model pin).
2. Send any execute request with policy=default, no --harness/--provider/--model.
3. Inspect the routing_decision event — only claude/opus-4.7 appears under claude; no sonnet, no haiku.
4. Resend with --model sonnet-4.6. Route goes to fiz/openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6 with score 196.0 (not to the claude subscription path), and fails 401 if openrouter is not authed.

CALLER CONTEXT
DDx CLI on main branch at commit 09881f3a5. DDx does not pre-resolve routing knobs (per CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010) and passes policy=default through verbatim. Verified by grep: no routing_decision/power_hint_fit/score_components emitter in DDx — only in github.com/easel/fizeau@v0.12.2.

NON-SCOPE
- The openrouter 401 (separate Fizeau config issue: that route needs an auth header configured).
- DDx-side changes; ddx work is doing the right thing per contract.
- Catalog metadata additions (sonnet-4.6 metadata is already complete).

CROSS-REF
Github issue: https://github.com/easel/fizeau/issues/6 (mirrors this bead for external visibility).
bead.acceptance:
1. Under policy=default with no --model pin, a claude subscription harness produces multiple candidates in routing_decision.candidates: one per available tier (at minimum opus-4.7 and sonnet-4.6; haiku if catalog has it). Verified by integration test that dispatches a default-policy request and asserts >1 claude candidate.
2. Each per-tier candidate carries the correct power and cost from internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml (sonnet=8, opus=10), and each routes to the same harness (claude), not via fiz/openrouter fallback.
3. For a 2000-3000-token implementation prompt at role=implementer with policy=default, sonnet-4.6 beats opus-4.7 on score under default scoring weights (cost_input_per_m: 3 vs 15 dominates the cost component). Verified by a deterministic scoring test or a snapshot of the routing_decision event with the chosen tier asserted.
4. Pinning --model sonnet-4.6 against the claude subscription harness routes through the claude harness (not fiz/openrouter). Verified by a test that sets up a claude harness mock and asserts dispatch destination.
5. The routing_decision event for any excluded same-harness tier carries an explicit filter_reason — silent absence of a candidate row is forbidden when the tier is known to the catalog. Verified by negative test: artificially break sonnet-4.6's catalog metadata and assert it appears with filter_reason:"power_missing" (or similar) rather than vanishing.
6. cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau && go test ./... green.
7. No regression in existing routing tests (service_adr005_test.go, service_routing_test.go, internal/routing tests).
